Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Moss is a pixel canvas where every brush is a tiny program 200 upvotes, 23 comments
  2. Flip Distance of Convex Triangulations and Tree Rotation Is NP-Complete 19 upvotes, 0 comments
  3. NanoGPT Slowrun: Language Modeling with Limited Data, Infinite Compute 132 upvotes, 26 comments
  4. Qwen3.5 Fine-Tuning Guide – Unsloth Documentation 295 upvotes, 70 comments
  5. Building a new Flash 374 upvotes, 105 comments
  6. Libre Solar – Open Hardware for Renewable Energy 221 upvotes, 62 comments
  7. MyFirst Kids Watch Hacked. Access to Camera and Microphone 119 upvotes, 32 comments
  8. “It turns out” (2010) 253 upvotes, 79 comments
  9. The View from RSS 83 upvotes, 22 comments
  10. An interactive map of Flock Cams 526 upvotes, 196 comments
  11. Daemon (2006) 27 upvotes, 5 comments
  12. Google Workspace CLI 190 upvotes, 71 comments
  13. Something is afoot in the land of Qwen 557 upvotes, 257 comments
  14. Dario Amodei calls OpenAI’s messaging around military deal ‘straight up lies’ 291 upvotes, 131 comments
  15. Raspberry Pi Pico as AM Radio Transmitter 75 upvotes, 30 comments
  16. A bit of fluid mechanics from scratch not from scratch 34 upvotes, 12 comments
  17. Humans 40k yrs ago developed a system of conventional signs 73 upvotes, 34 comments
  18. Chaos and Dystopian news for the dead internet survivors 36 upvotes, 14 comments
  19. NRC Issues First Commercial Reactor Construction Approval in 10 Years [pdf] 60 upvotes, 28 comments
  20. Glaze by Raycast 202 upvotes, 123 comments
  21. Malm Whale in Gothenburg 8 upvotes, 1 comments
  22. Picking Up a Zillion Pieces of Litter 47 upvotes, 25 comments
  23. What Python's asyncio primitives get wrong about shared state 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  24. I left work early to get a hug from my wife 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  25. Was Windows 1.0's lack of overlapping windows a legal or a technical matter? 61 upvotes, 41 comments
  26. Data Has Weight but Only on SSDs 74 upvotes, 58 comments
  27. BMW Group to deploy humanoid robots in production in Germany for the first time 93 upvotes, 76 comments
  28. US tech firms pledge at White House to bear costs of energy for datacenters 28 upvotes, 20 comments
  29. MacBook Neo 1614 upvotes, 1929 comments
  30. What's Driving Rising Business Costs? 20 upvotes, 26 comments

Last generated 11:30am, 5 March 2026. Source here.
